title: Senecio lasiorhizoides
taxonomy: Senecio lasiorhizoides is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Senecio, which consists of various species of flowering plants. This species is native to Australia and is often found in coastal regions. The classification of Senecio lasiorhizoides is further categorized as a member of the order Asterales. Further classification places it in the class Magnoliopsida, subclass Asteridae, and clade Rosidae within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Senecio lasiorhizoides, also known as woolly ragwort, is a perennial plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes Mountains. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Senecio lasiorhizoides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Senecio lasiorhizoides is often made into a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory infections.
